what_it_does: Bootstraps the React application: imports global styles (tldraw, the shared design-system, and local app.css), then creates a React 18 root and renders the <App/> component inside <React.StrictMode>.
observations: Imports 'tldraw/tldraw.css' before the design-system and app styles.
Imports the shared design-system CSS via a relative path ('../../../design/design-system.css') and a local './app.css'.
Contains a detailed comment (F1) explaining that design-system :root tokens load before app.css so app.css can alias legacy variables onto corpus tokens.
Uses ReactDOM.createRoot with a non-null assertion on document.getElementById('root').
Wraps the rendered tree in React.StrictMode.
No exported code symbols are defined in this file.
standouts: Explicit, commented CSS token-loading strategy tying a shared design-system to local app.css.
tldraw CSS is loaded as a global dependency at the application entry point.
React 18 createRoot API is used rather than the legacy ReactDOM.render.
conventions: Standard React 18 entry-point pattern with createRoot and StrictMode.
Global CSS import ordering is intentional and documented.
Relative path imports for shared and local styles.
TypeScript non-null assertion for the root DOM element.
concerns: Assumes the 'root' DOM element exists at runtime with no fallback.
CSS load order is fragile: reordering imports could break the token-aliasing contract described in F1.
No runtime error handling around root creation or rendering.
notes: This is the React 18 entry point for the canvas app, responsible for loading the global style hierarchy and mounting App.
questions: What exact legacy CSS variables does app.css alias onto the corpus tokens?
What does the App component render and how does it use tldraw?
Is the 'root' element guaranteed to exist in the host HTML for all deployment contexts?
Why is tldraw CSS imported before the design-system CSS rather than after?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a standard React application entry point and its imports align with the documented goal of loading the design-system token layer before app.css.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a single, shared visual-design token system for the canvas app while bootstrapping a tldraw-based React UI.
novelty: The inline F1 comment documents the CSS import-order rationale and the token-aliasing contract, making the style-loading intent explicit in the entry point.
contribution: Provides the application bootstrap sequence, the documented global CSS dependency order, and the root React render call that mounts the top-level component.
summary_for_embedding: main.tsx is the React 18 entry point for the canvas app, importing tldraw and the shared design-system/app global styles before rendering <App/> inside React.StrictMode.
intention_for_embedding: To initialize the canvas application's DOM root, establish the global CSS token hierarchy, and mount the top-level React component.
